system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices

    (9 1 2)       // 6

);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (90 10 20) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    topWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
    bottomWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }
    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }

        faces

        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,R)      Gauss upwind;
    div(R)          Gauss linear;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p_rgh
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    p_rghFinal
    {
        $p_rgh;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is clear:

```
Cannot find patchField entry for topWall
```

This occurs in the `0/T` file. Looking at the current `0/T` file, the boundary patches defined are:
- `upperWall`
- `lowerWall`
- `leftWall`
- `rightWall`
- `frontAndBack`

However, the `blockMeshDict` defines these patches:
- `topWall`
- `bottomWall`
- `leftWall`
- `rightWall`
- `frontWall`
- `backWall`

**All six patches from `blockMeshDict` must be present in every boundary field file.** The `0/T` file is missing entries for `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `frontWall`, and `backWall`.

---

## Proposed Fixes

### 1. Fix `0/T` — Add missing patch entries

Add entries for `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `frontWall`, and `backWall` to the `boundaryField` section. Based on the user requirement ("One wall has a temperature of 301 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K"), set:

- **`topWall`** (the 301 K wall): `fixedValue` with value `uniform 301`
- **`bottomWall`** (the 300 K wall): `fixedValue` with value `uniform 300`
- **`frontWall`**: `zeroGradient`
- **`backWall`**: `zeroGradient`

Remove the incorrect `upperWall` and `lowerWall` entries entirely.

### 2. Fix `0/U` — Same issue

The `0/U` file also uses `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `frontAndBack` which don't match the `blockMeshDict` patches. Replace with proper entries matching all six patches (`topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all set to `noSlip`.

### 3. Fix `0/k` — Same issue

Replace `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontAndBack` with all six patches: `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. Set appropriate wall-function types.

### 4. Fix `0/nut` — Same issue

Replace the patch names to match all six patches from `blockMeshDict`.

### 5. Fix `0/alphat` — Same issue

Replace patch names to match all six patches.

### 6. Fix `0/epsilon` — Same issue

Replace patch names to match all six patches.

### 7. Fix `0/p` — Same issue

Replace patch names to match all six patches.

### 8. Fix `0/p_rgh` — Already correct

The `p_rgh` file already includes all six patches (`topWall`, `bottomWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`), so it is fine.

---

## Summary

Every boundary condition file in the `0/` directory must have exactly the same six patch names as defined in `blockMeshDict`: `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, and `backWall`. Currently, several files use mismatched patch names (`upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `frontAndBack`) which causes the runtime error. Each file needs to be updated to use the correct patch names with appropriate boundary condition types consistent with the Bernard Cells tutorial setup.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t=1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 301 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
